_comment: Schema example for an AI Context data dict (v0.17 — HubSpot's 2026-05-28 reorg). The skill protocol writes this file as `output/[slug]-data.json` during Phase 5; the build script (build_company_context_html.py) consumes it to produce the Business + Brand Kit tabs of the HTML paste sheet. ICPs and Personas come from separate CSV files passed via --icps and --personas. Every key below maps to a HubSpot AI Context field documented in references/hubspot-company-context-fields.md. Leave a field empty ("" or []) when synthesis can't derive it — the build script renders empty fields as a visible [Confirm with client] placeholder rather than dropping them. Never fabricate a value to fill a field.
business_description: One to three sentences describing what the company does and who it serves. First-person plural (we/our) when the client speaks that way on their own site. Operational specificity over hype.
unique_value_propositions: One UVP per tag — 10-15 tags typical
Self-serve onboarding with no implementation fee
Native warehouse connectors for Snowflake and BigQuery
business_model: Subscription SaaS with usage-based tiers and annual contracts.
primary_business_goal: Help mid-market teams make decisions from their own data without a data-engineering hire.
mission: Business-profile Mission, one paragraph capped at 50 words. May differ from the Brand Kit Mission below — HubSpot treats the two surfaces independently.
mission_brandkit: Brand Kit Mission (≤ 50 words). Optional — if present, the Brand Kit tab renders this instead of `mission`. Omit this key entirely to fall back to `mission` for both surfaces.
vision: Aspirational future-state statement. NEW field in the 2026-05-28 reorg, separate from Mission. Pull from About or leadership pages.
social_responsibility: Each social-cause or community-impact theme as one tag — 8-12 typical
positive_associations: From Phase 3.5 positive findings — testimonials, trade-press wins, third-party praise
Responsive support consistently praised in reviews
negative_associations: From Phase 3.5 negative findings — complaints, friction signals
Pricing opacity above the top published tier (recurring G2 complaint)
If nothing surfaces after a thorough pass, write: No significant negative associations found in public web as of [Month YYYY]
_section_market: --- Business tab → Market and ecosystem context ---
stakeholders: NEW field — non-competitor parties that influence the business
existing_apps_and_tools: Business-facing apps the company uses or integrates
tech_stack_key_tools: Web/cloud infrastructure — Breeze auto-detects these, often lowercase_with_underscores
_section_products: --- Business tab → Products and services (structured DB, one record per product) ---

products_db.name: Meridian Dashboards
products_db.description: Concise descriptor-style text — what the product is and what it does, in one line to one short paragraph. The full per-product depth lives in output/[slug]-products.md.

products_db.name: Meridian Pipelines
products_db.description: Managed ETL connectors that sync source systems into the customer's warehouse on a schedule.

_section_brandkit: --- Brand Kit tab (HubSpot's separate Brand Kit surface, linked from the Business tab) ---
replacement_note: Notes on when these rules should fire (e.g., speech-to-text artifacts from internal calls render the brand as X; auto-correct to Y).
_section_openitems: --- Brand Kit tab → Open Items (client-confirm checklist) ---
open_items: NPS score — actual score from the client (not derivable from the website)
Inclusivity preferences — confirm the 3 Brand Kit toggle settings (gender-neutral, cultural, idioms)
Picklist validations — flag any Personality / Default Tone / Company size / Annual revenue values that may need closest-fit override against HubSpot's actual UI
Mission sync — confirm whether the Business-profile Mission and Brand Kit Mission should match or differ
Anything specific to this client — open data gaps the client must close
